title: onobrychis supina subsp. intermedia
intro: Onobrychis supina subsp. intermedia, also known as intermediate sainfoin, is a plant species within the pea family and is closely related to the common sainfoin (Onobrychis sativa). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and stems contain bioactive compounds that provide health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Onobrychis supina subsp. intermedia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, making it a valuable addition to traditional herbal medicine.
